ruleset.name: Exalted 3rd Edition
ruleset.license: Original mechanics references; charm names belong to Onyx Path Publishing. No verbatim Onyx Path text reproduced.
ruleset.summary: Storyteller-system d10 dice pools. Pool = Attribute + Ability. Successes on 7+. Tens double on PC/major-NPC rolls. Botch on a roll that shows zero successes AND at least one 1.

resolution.poolFormula: Attribute + Ability (+ specialty +1, + stunt up to +2, + Charm dice)

Routine.description: Trivial for a competent character. Often waived.

Demanding.description: Few mortals can do it reliably.

Legendary.description: Peak of mortal-scale achievement; songs are sung.

Beyond Legendary.description: Wyld navigation, First-Age relics, Essence > 5 territory.

attributes.name: Appearance
attributes.description: Beauty / presence; affects attraction and intimidation.

skills.name: Brawl
skills.description: Includes Martial Arts when the Martial Artist Merit is taken.

derivedStats.name: Willpower
derivedStats.formula: Per-character cap (typically 5-10 for Solars; max 10). User sets the cap via the max input on the bar; default starts at 5 for fresh Solar sheets.

derivedStats.name: Health Track
derivedStats.formula: 7 levels: -0/-1/-1/-2/-2/-4/Incapacitated. Penalty equals the highest filled box.

damageTypes.label: B
damageTypes.description: Blunt or non-lethal. Heals quickly. Pushed to the right when worse damage stacks on top.

damageTypes.label: L
damageTypes.description: Edged or piercing. Slow to heal. Sits between bashing and aggravated.

damageTypes.label: A
damageTypes.description: Soulsteel, fire, dragon-blood claws. Heals only with magical aid. Always leftmost in the damage stack.

derivedStats.name: Defense (Parry)
derivedStats.formula: (Dexterity + Melee) / 2, round up; armor and Charms apply

derivedStats.name: Defense (Evasion)
derivedStats.formula: (Dexterity + Dodge) / 2, round up; armor penalty applies

derivedStats.name: Join Battle
derivedStats.formula: Wits + Awareness (dice pool; on declare-attack first action)

derivedStats.name: Soak (Natural)
derivedStats.formula: Stamina (Exalts use full Stamina; mortals halve, round down)

derivedStats.name: Soak (Lethal)
derivedStats.formula: Stamina + armor L-soak (Exalts use FULL Stamina; mortals use Stamina/2 rounded down)

derivedStats.name: Hardness
derivedStats.formula: Highest equipped armor's Hardness rating + Charm/magic Hardness bonuses (set base manually; armor and Charms appear as +N suffix from item bonuses targeting "Hardness")

values.label: Suppressed
values.description: Anima banner is suppressed or unlit. No outward flare; caste mark dormant. Use when the Exalt has spent no Peripheral motes this action, is intentionally damping the banner (Charm or stunt), or is a non-Exalt who simply has no anima.

values.label: Dim
values.trigger: 0-4 Peripheral motes spent in one action; only the caste mark glints when motes are spent.

values.label: Glowing
values.trigger: 5-10 Peripheral motes spent in one action; faint nimbus, caste mark steady.

values.label: Burning
values.trigger: 11-15 Peripheral motes spent in one action; bright glow, surrounding flicker.

values.label: Bonfire
values.trigger: 16+ Peripheral motes spent in one action; massive radiance, illuminates the scene.

values.label: Iconic
values.trigger: Sustained Bonfire or specific Charm triggers; full totemic spirit-shape.

values.label: 1-die
values.description: Vivid, environment-aware. Reward: +2 dice OR +1 to a static value.

values.label: 2-die
values.description: Genuinely creative, integrates fiction. Reward: +2 dice OR +2 static, plus 1 mote OR 1 Willpower.

values.label: 3-die
values.description: Spontaneous, table-applauds. Reward: +2 dice OR +3 static, plus 2 motes OR 2 Willpower (can exceed cap). Rare by GM design.

values.label: None
values.description: Has not yet learned any Sorcery (or is not a sorcerer).

values.label: Terrestrial
values.description: Emerald Circle. Open to Solars, Lunars, Sidereals, Dragon-Blooded, Liminals, and rare mortals. Foundation tier.

values.label: Celestial
values.description: Sapphire Circle. Solars, Lunars, Sidereals only. Higher mysteries; cannot be learned without first mastering Terrestrial.

values.label: Solar
values.description: Adamant Circle. Solar Exalted only. The peak of mortal sorcerous power; legends and rumors only since the Sun's Chosen disappeared.

values.label: Shaping
values.description: A spell is being shaped. The spell name should be tracked as a 'Shaping: <spell name>' condition; sorcerous motes accumulate in the Sorcerous Motes counter; 1 Willpower is committed up front and refunded only on successful cast.

values.label: Active
values.description: Initiative is at 1 or higher. The character is operating normally in combat — withering attacks add to their Initiative, decisive attacks may be declared, and standard Join Battle ordering applies.

values.label: Crashed
values.trigger: Initiative drops to 0 or below as a result of withering damage or specific Charm effects.
values.description: Initiative crashed. The character cannot make decisive attacks until they reset to base, suffers Onslaught penalties more readily, and is the prime target for finishing decisive attacks. Reset to base Initiative occurs after 3 turns (or 5 if crashed below -5), at which point the Crash Recovery bonus applies and the character returns to Active.

vocabularyHints.hint: In Exalted, parry uses Parry DV (Static value; no roll needed unless adding charms). Note the character's stored Parry DV and any active charm modifiers.
In Exalted, dodge uses Evasion DV (Static value; no roll). Note Evasion DV; subtract -1 onslaught penalty for each successive attack this round.
In Exalted, an attack rolls Attribute + Ability + accuracy bonus + Charm dice. Mention the relevant pool and check whether the player is invoking any Charms before resolving.

Track the spend separately for Personal and Peripheral pools. Personal-only Charms must come from Personal motes; mixed Charms allow the player to choose. Update anima banner if Peripheral spend exceeds threshold.
Exalted uses Join Battle (Wits + Awareness) at combat start; initiative is then a fluid tracker that rises/falls with combat actions. Don't roll a fresh JB if combat is already underway.

lorebook.name: Exalted 3e Reference
lorebook.description: Keyword-triggered reference for Exalted 3rd Edition (2016 Onyx Path core) mechanics: anima, motes, stunts, willpower, sample Solar charms. Original mechanics references; charm names belong to Onyx Path Publishing.

entries.name: Rule: Dice-pool resolution
entries.content: Resolution: roll a pool of d10s equal to (Attribute + Ability). Each 7, 8, 9, or 10 is one success. Each 10 counts as TWO successes (tens-double). Specialties: +1 die when applicable. Stunts add up to +2 dice (cap regardless of tier). Charms can add dice or change the rule. Difficulty is the success count needed (1=Routine, 2=Standard, 3=Difficult, 4=Demanding, 5=Legendary). Extras (mooks) do NOT double tens.

entries.name: Rule: Botch
entries.content: Botch: a roll producing zero successes AND at least one die showing 1 is a botch. The GM introduces a complication, not just a miss — the action fails in a memorably bad way. Note: 1s do NOT subtract from successes; they only matter when successes are zero.

entries.name: Rule: Stunts
entries.content: Stunts. The GM grades the player's narration: 1-die (vivid, environment-aware) = +2 dice OR +1 static. 2-die (genuinely creative, integrates fiction) = +2 dice OR +2 static, plus 1 mote OR 1 Willpower. 3-die (spontaneous, table-applauds) = +2 dice OR +3 static, plus 2 motes OR 2 Willpower (can exceed cap). Dice bonus caps at +2 across all tiers; the higher tier increases the static bonus and the regen reward.

entries.name: Rule: Anima banner
entries.content: Anima banner. Spending Peripheral motes (NOT Personal) flares the anima: +1 banner level per 5 Peripheral motes spent in a single action. Levels: Dim (caste mark glints when motes spent) -> Glowing (5-10 spent, faint nimbus) -> Burning (11-15, bright glow) -> Bonfire (16+, illuminates the scene) -> Iconic (sustained Bonfire or Charm trigger, full totemic spirit-shape). Anima displays make stealth impossible at higher tiers and have social/magical implications.

entries.name: Rule: Motes (Solar)
entries.content: Motes. Solar Personal pool = Essence x 3 + 10 (subtle, no anima flare). Solar Peripheral pool = Essence x 7 + 26 (overt, flares anima). Spend Personal first by default. In combat, regenerate 5 motes per round automatically. Out of combat, mote regen is GM-discretion based on activity (typically 0/4/8 per hour for strenuous/light/restful activity). Essence rating caps Charm prerequisites and pool size.

entries.name: Rule: Willpower
entries.content: Willpower. Max 10. Solars start at 5. Spend 1 WP for +1 automatic success OR +1 to a static value (Resolve, Guile, Defense), once per roll/contest, declared BEFORE the roll. Also spent to power some Charms and resist mental influence. Regain +1 per full night's sleep (cap permanent rating). Regain +1 per scene when upholding a Major or Defining Intimacy through significant hardship (max 1/scene). Regain +1 (2-die stunt) or +2 (3-die stunt); these CAN exceed permanent rating.

entries.name: Rule: Health track
entries.content: Health track: 7 levels — -0, -1, -1, -2, -2, -4, Incapacitated. The wound penalty in effect equals the HIGHEST filled box and applies to most dice pools and static values. Bashing damage at Incapacitated -> KO until end of scene. Lethal/Aggravated at Incapacitated -> unconscious up to 1 day; an Exalt does NOT auto-die. Dying levels = Stamina; while dying, take 1 lethal per action until stabilized or dead.

entries.name: Rule: Intimacies
entries.content: Intimacies. Emotional/ideological commitments shaping the character's choices and resistance. Two kinds: Ties (toward a person/place/group) and Principles (abstract beliefs). Three intensities: Minor, Major, Defining. They affect Resolve in social influence: a Tie/Principle that supports the influence makes acceptance easier; one that opposes it makes it harder (or impossible without overcoming the Intimacy first). Major/Defining Intimacies are the source of regained Willpower under hardship.

entries.name: Rule: Charm anatomy
entries.content: Charm anatomy. Cost: motes (e.g., 5m), often + Willpower (1wp), sometimes + anima/initiative/health. Type: Simple (uses your action; one Simple per turn), Supplemental (enhances another action; many per round, no stacking same Charm), Reflexive (interrupt-style trigger), Permanent (passive, always-on once bought). Duration: Instant / One scene / Until next [X] / Indefinite / Permanent. Prerequisites: prior Charms in the tree plus an Essence minimum (typically 1-5).

entries.name: Charm: Excellent Strike
entries.content: Excellent Strike — Solar Melee Charm. Bedrock offensive Charm: adds dice to a Melee attack and makes the attack roll harder for opponents to dodge or parry. Most Solar Melee Charms branch from this in the cascade.

entries.name: Charm: Dipping Swallow Defense
entries.content: Dipping Swallow Defense — Solar Melee Charm, Reflexive. Reflexively wipes most penalties to your Parry against an incoming attack, letting you defend at full effectiveness when surprised or wounded.

entries.name: Charm: Graceful Crane Stance
entries.content: Graceful Crane Stance — Solar Athletics Charm. Lets the Solar balance perfectly on any surface, no matter how thin, slick, or unstable, for the scene. Walking on rooftops, tightropes, the surface of water (briefly) — no balance check required.

entries.name: Charm: Monkey Leap Technique
entries.content: Monkey Leap Technique — Solar Athletics Charm. Lets the Solar jump an entire range band (short, medium, long) as a movement action — clearing gaps and walls that mortals would need climbing gear for.

entries.name: Charm: Hardship-Surviving Mendicant Spirit
entries.content: Hardship-Surviving Mendicant Spirit — Solar Survival Charm. Negates environmental penalties for the scene: extreme cold, heat, thin air, exhaustion. The Solar pushes through deserts, blizzards, and sleepless weeks at full effectiveness.

entries.name: Charm: Judge's Ear Technique
entries.content: Judge's Ear Technique — Solar Investigation Charm, Reflexive. When the Solar hears speech, the Charm reflexively reveals whether the words are a lie, a half-truth, or sincere. Does not pierce magical concealment of intent.

entries.name: Charm: Flawless Pickpocketing Technique
entries.content: Flawless Pickpocketing Technique — Solar Larceny Charm. Auto-succeeds a touch-range theft against a mundane (non-Charm-defended) target. The mark feels nothing.

entries.name: Charm: Lock-Opening Touch
entries.content: Lock-Opening Touch — Solar Larceny Charm. Opens any non-magical lock — door, chest, manacle — at a touch. No tools required, no time spent.

entries.name: Rule: Caste, Favored, Supernal
entries.content: Solar PCs choose a Caste (Dawn/Zenith/Twilight/Night/Eclipse), each with 5 Caste Abilities chosen from a fixed pool of 8 caste-flavored options. The PC additionally picks 5 Favored Abilities from anywhere on the list. Caste + Favored Abilities (10 total) get cheaper Charm purchase costs and free Excellency access. One of those 10 is designated Supernal: that Ability is treated as Essence 5 for Charm-prerequisite purposes, allowing capstone Charms in that one Ability from Essence 1.

entries.name: Rule: Sorcery — The Three Circles
entries.content:
Sorcery is divided into three Circles of understanding and power. Old-Realm names: Terrestrial, Celestial, Solar. Realm-trained sorcerers call them Emerald, Sapphire, and Adamant.

Who can learn what:
- Terrestrial Circle: Solars, Lunars, Sidereals, Dragon-Blooded, Liminals, and (rarely) mortals.
- Celestial Circle: Solars, Lunars, Sidereals only. Requires Terrestrial first.
- Solar Circle: Solar Exalted only. Requires Terrestrial AND Celestial first. Dormant in legend since the Sun's Chosen disappeared.
- Abyssals wield equivalent power as necromancy (different fluff, parallel mechanics).

Progression: Solars start by mastering Terrestrial via the Terrestrial Circle Sorcery Charm (p. 360 of the core book). Higher Circles require subsequent Charms in their respective sorcery cascades. Each Circle unlocks access to spells of that tier; a Celestial sorcerer can still cast Terrestrial spells.

entries.name: Rule: Sorcery — Shape Sorcery Action and Casting
entries.content:
Sorcerous motes do NOT come from the sorcerer's Personal or Peripheral pools. They are gathered from the ambient flow of Essence around her by taking Shape Sorcery actions.

Shape Sorcery action (combat action — cannot be flurried):
1. The sorcerer DECLARES the spell she is shaping.
2. She rolls (Intelligence + Occult). Each success = 1 sorcerous mote toward that spell's cost.
3. If accumulated sorcerous motes >= the spell's cost, she UNLEASHES the spell immediately and reflexively.
4. Otherwise, she may continue shaping next round, accumulating more motes.

Leakage: If the sorcerer does anything other than gather motes during a round, she loses 3 sorcerous motes at the end of that round. If she stops gathering entirely, she keeps losing 3/round until the pool is empty — at which point the spell is lost. A ritual that lets her reflexively gather motes counts as a Shape Sorcery action (sorcerer-assassins use these to fight while shaping).

Switching spells: If she begins shaping a different spell, the current spell aborts immediately and all gathered sorcerous motes disperse and are lost.

Willpower: Most spells cost at least 1 Willpower, paid up front when shaping begins. If the spell is cast successfully, 1 Willpower is RESTORED — the act of completing the design fulfills her. If the spell is aborted, lost, or countered, that Willpower is simply spent.

Ritual cost: Some spells require hours or days of shaping. These are noted with a cost of "Ritual"; track elapsed time rather than per-round mote accumulation.

Identifying a sorcery spell: in the spellbook, a spell will name its Circle (Terrestrial / Celestial / Solar) and its sorcerous-mote cost. If the cost is given in plain motes (5m, 10m), it is a Charm — use the standard Charm cost flow (Personal/Peripheral motes), NOT shape sorcery.

entries.name: Rule: Extras (mooks)
entries.content: Extras (mooks) are simplified opponents. They have a single combined trait (often Drill or quality rating), often die after one Health Level of damage, and their 10s do NOT double on rolls (unlike PCs and major NPCs). Use them when the fiction calls for a crowd of nameless threats; promote any to Battle Group or full opponent if the fiction demands.

entries.name: Optional Sub-Agents — what they do and how to enable
entries.content:
The Marinara-RPG ruleset bundle ships five OPTIONAL pre-generation sub-agents that focus the main narration model on specific tasks. They are installed DISABLED by default — flip them on individually in Marinara's Settings → Agents panel. Each adds one model call per turn while enabled, so enable only what your campaign needs.

The five sub-agents:

1. **state-mutator** — Instructs the narration model to emit hidden `[mrr-state: ...]` tags inside narration when the story changes a character's HP, conditions, or inventory. The extension parses these tags, applies the change to the floating sheet, and shows a confirmation toast. Without this enabled, you have to mutate the sheet by hand. Enable when: you want narration to drive the sheet automatically.

2. **state-reminder** — Surfaces a short bulleted list of current player character state (HP, conditions, equipped gear, tracked resources) at the top of every turn so the narration model stays mechanically honest between messages. Enable when: long sessions or complex sheets, the model is forgetting your stats.

3. **combat-adjudicator** — Wakes up only when combat is active. Restates initiative, action economy, attack/damage formulas, and conditions in the active ruleset's terms before each turn. Outputs nothing (literally "No combat active.") in social or ambient scenes. Enable when: you run heavy-mechanics combat encounters.

4. **lore-query** — Wakes up only when the latest user message is a rules question (e.g., "How does grappling work?", "What's the DC for picking a lock?"). Answers from the installed lorebook + system RAW. Outputs nothing ("No rules query.") otherwise. Enable when: you frequently ask the model rules questions mid-RP.

5. **npc-bookkeeper** — Tracks active and recently-engaged NPC HP, conditions, tactical state, and telegraphed intentions across turns. Outputs nothing ("No NPCs to track.") when no NPCs are in scene. Enable when: combat or social scenes with multiple named NPCs you want continuity on.

How to enable: Marinara → Settings → Agents → find the agent (named like "MRR: <System> — <Role>" for GM-mode, "MRRP: ..." for RP-mode) → toggle on. The agent persists across chats; toggle off any time.

entries.name: Rule: Hardness
entries.content: Hardness is a damage floor on armor and certain Charms or supernatural defenses. When an attack's post-soak raw damage total is LESS THAN the defender's Hardness, the damage is reduced to the attacker's weapon Overwhelming value (rather than what the soak would otherwise produce). Hardness does NOT add to soak; it is a separate threshold. Mortal armor in Exalted typically has 0 Hardness; Essence-charged Charms and artifact armor grant Hardness 1+. Higher-Essence Charms can confer Hardness ratings of 5, 7, or higher.

entries.name: Rule: Overwhelming damage
entries.content: Overwhelming is the minimum levels of damage a weapon or attack always inflicts, even when soak or Hardness would otherwise reduce it lower. Default Overwhelming for unarmed and light weapons is 1; medium weapons 2; heavy weapons 3; many artifact weapons 4-5. When you compute final damage: roll soak versus damage as normal, but the result can never drop below the attacker's Overwhelming. When Hardness blocks (raw damage < Hardness), the floor that lands is exactly Overwhelming. Charms and artifact qualities can raise an attack's Overwhelming value.

entries.name: Rule: Intimacies (Ties and Principles)
entries.content: Intimacies are an Exalt's emotional and ideological anchors. Two kinds: TIES (a strong feeling toward an external subject — person, place, organization, idea) and PRINCIPLES (an internal credo or belief). Three intensity degrees: MINOR (a soft preference), MAJOR (a real commitment that shapes behavior), DEFINING (an identity-level conviction). Defining Intimacies provide outright immunity to Decision-Influence social attacks that contradict them; Major Intimacies can be invoked to resist such attacks at higher Willpower cost; Minor Intimacies bend in play. Story actions that align with an Intimacy regain Willpower; betraying a Defining Intimacy can trigger Limit gain or breakdown for the Solar Exalted. Intimacies evolve through play and certain Charms can shift their degree or replace them.

entries.name: Mechanic: Mote Commitment
entries.content:
Mote commitment locks a portion of the Exalt Essence pool to sustain a persistent effect — an attuned artifact, an active Charm with a Reflexively Maintained duration, an atemi committed to a defensive stance. Motes spent this way are unavailable for any other use until commitment is released. Two pools: Personal (small, always-available, 5/Essence rating motes for self-sustaining effects) and Peripheral (larger, refilled by drawing on the anima banner — outward expressions of power). Commitment subtracts from the named pool; release restores. Pool floor is 0 — the Exalt cannot commit motes they do not have. Switching commitment between pools (Personal → Peripheral) restores the old commit to the old pool first, then debits the new commit from the new pool atomically.

State-mutator tag: [mrrp-state: field="commitment" item="<artifact name>" motes="<int>" pool="Personal|Peripheral"]. motes="0" releases. Pool floor and exclusivity (mote-committed items cannot be attuned/invested) are enforced at parse time; rejection surfaces as a toast and leaves the sheet unchanged.

entries.name: Rule: Character creation flow (Solar)
entries.content: Solar PCs are built in eight steps. 1) Concept & Caste (Dawn/Zenith/Twilight/Night/Eclipse). 2) Attributes — split 9 dots across the Primary category, 7 across the Secondary, 5 across the Tertiary (Physical/Social/Mental), every Attribute starts at 1. 3) Abilities — 28 dots over 25 Abilities, max 3 in any one, at least one Caste/Favored Ability at 3. 4) Merits — 10 dots from the Merit lists. 5) Charms — 15 Charms (≥10 must be Solar Charms; up to 5 Martial Arts or other-non-Caste). 6) Intimacies — one Defining plus four Minor/Major. 7) Bonus Points — 15 BP to round out. 8) Finishing Touches — Essence 1, Willpower 5, Limit 0, pick a Limit Trigger, designate the Supernal Ability, derive Health/Initiative/Defense/Resolve/Guile.

entries.name: Rule: Bonus Point costs at creation
entries.content: Bonus Point costs (Solar, 15 BP total). Attribute = 4 BP (3 if Caste/Favored). Caste or Favored Ability = 1 BP. Non-Caste/Favored Ability = 2 BP. Specialty = 1 BP. Merit dot = 1 BP. Willpower dot = 2 BP. Caste/Favored Charm = 4 BP, non-Caste Charm = 5 BP. Spell in the sorcerer's highest Circle = 4 BP if Occult is Caste/Favored, 5 if not. Mortals build with 21 BP, cap 7 Merit dots, and get no Charms.

entries.name: Rule: Specialties
entries.content: A Specialty is a narrow application of an Ability (Melee: Daiklaves; Socialize: Court Intrigue; Survival: The Wyld). When it applies to the current roll, +1 die. Up to three Specialties per Ability, each bought separately. Specialty also feeds derived static values — Resolve and Guile include +1 if a Specialty applies in context.

entries.name: Rule: Experienced Solar build
entries.content: An Experienced Solar starts closer to mid-arc: 13 Merits, 20 Charms (one may be Solar Circle Sorcery if Essence 3+), Essence 2, and 18 Bonus Points. Use when the troupe wants to skip the freshly-Exalted opening or when the campaign concept demands veterans. All other creation rules apply normally.

entries.name: Rule: Caste Abilities — full reference
entries.content: Each Caste has 8 Caste Abilities; the player picks 5 of those as Caste, then 5 anywhere on the list as Favored. Dawn: Archery, Awareness, Brawl/Martial Arts, Dodge, Melee, Resistance, Thrown, War. Zenith: Athletics, Integrity, Performance, Lore, Presence, Resistance, Survival, War. Twilight: Bureaucracy, Craft, Integrity, Investigation, Linguistics, Lore, Medicine, Occult. Night: Athletics, Awareness, Dodge, Investigation, Larceny, Ride, Stealth, Socialize. Eclipse: Bureaucracy, Larceny, Linguistics, Occult, Presence, Ride, Sail, Socialize. Caste + Favored (10 total) cost less in Charm purchase. One Caste Ability is the Supernal — treated as Essence 5 for Charm prerequisites.

entries.name: Rule: Caste Anima Effects (all five)
entries.content: Each Caste has signature anima-banner effects, paid in motes once anima reaches Burning (11+ Peripheral spent). DAWN — pay 10m to make presence a weapon; enemies who see her suffer Resolve/Initiative-shifting morale penalties, scaling to terror at Iconic. ZENITH — pay 10m once per scene for aggravated damage against the undead/Creatures of Darkness, or to consecrate corpses against rising; her anima beacons inspire mortals and purge minor undead at Iconic. TWILIGHT — pay 10m for soak equal to Essence rating until next turn (defensive radiant cocoon), often extending to allies at Iconic. NIGHT — only Caste whose anima can be SUPPRESSED at Glowing (1m extra per stealth-mote spent) to stay hidden; at higher tiers Night uses anima tricks to confuse witnesses about who struck. ECLIPSE — pay 10m + 1wp to sanctify a binding oath (oath-breakers suffer divine retribution); also grants Heaven-recognized diplomatic safe-passage against spirits and gods.

entries.name: Rule: The nine Attributes
entries.content: Nine Attributes in three categories. PHYSICAL — Strength (raw power, Feats of Strength), Dexterity (agility, attacks, dodge), Stamina (endurance, soak, resist poison/disease). SOCIAL — Charisma (honest leadership, inspiration), Manipulation (deceit, persuasion against the speaker's interest), Appearance (visual presence, often gates social reach). MENTAL — Perception (noticing, ambush detection), Intelligence (analysis, problem-solving, sorcery shaping), Wits (reflexes, Join Battle, quick thinking). Attributes cap at 5 in play (Charms can effectively exceed). Each starts at 1.

entries.name: Rule: The 25 Abilities (reference)
entries.content: The 25 Abilities: Archery, Athletics, Awareness, Brawl, Bureaucracy, Craft, Dodge, Integrity, Investigation, Larceny, Linguistics, Lore, Martial Arts, Medicine, Melee, Occult, Performance, Presence, Resistance, Ride, Sail, Socialize, Stealth, Survival, Thrown, War. Brawl and Martial Arts can both be rated, but Solar Charm cascades branch into Brawl OR Martial Arts trees. Linguistics 1 = one Native + (Linguistics) additional languages. Lore covers academic knowledge, history, and spirit identification. Resistance powers soak, willpower regen, and endurance Charms.

entries.name: Rule: Merit categories
entries.content: Merits are extra traits beyond Attributes and Abilities. Three categories. INNATE — biological/natural (Strong Lungs, Pain Tolerance, Iron Stomach, Eidetic Memory); cannot be purchased mid-arc, cannot be lost in play. PURCHASED — acquired training or possessions (Resources, Fast Reflexes, Quick Draw, Ambidextrous); bought with BP or XP, may be lost narratively. STORY — defined by ongoing fiction (Allies, Contacts, Mentor, Followers, Manse, Cult); can grow or be lost via play. Merit dots typically run 1-5. Some Merits gate other character options (Manse provides a Hearthstone; Artifact provides equipment that requires attunement).

entries.name: Rule: Common Merits sampler
entries.content: Commonly-taken Merits. Resources (1-5) — cash and material wealth, gates lifestyle and Equipment access. Allies (1-5) — characters who provide aid, ranked by capability. Backing — formal rank within an organization. Mentor — a more powerful figure who teaches or advises. Followers — a group of devoted retainers. Contacts — a network providing information. Cult — worshipers who provide motes through devotion (1 = small shrine, 3 = local cult). Manse (1-5) — a place of geomantic power, generates a Hearthstone of equal rating. Demesne — wild geomantic site without a manse; may aid sorcery. Hearthstone — focused crystal granting specific magical benefits, dot-rated. Artifact (1-5) — a magical item; must be attuned (commit motes) to use. Influence — political pull within a polity or institution.

entries.name: Rule: Supernatural Merits
entries.content: Supernatural Merits represent non-mortal biology or limited innate magic. Examples: Wings (small = glide; full = flight). Claws/Fangs/Hooves/Horns (natural weapons, stat as light or medium). Wall Walking (cling to vertical surfaces without rolls). Tail (extra grasping limb). Gills (breathe water). Venomous (natural toxin, stat as a Damage/Interval poison). Enhanced Sense (one specific sense at superhuman acuity). Quills, Unusual Hide, Poisoned Body, Subtlety, Chameleon, Extra Limbs, Exalted Healing (mortals only). Thaumaturgist — limited mundane magic outside the Sorcery system. Usually restricted to non-human Exalts, beastfolk, or ST-approved exceptions.

entries.name: Rule: Flaws & Derangements
entries.content: Flaws are persistent negative traits that grant a small BP refund at character creation (typically 2-4 BP each). Examples: Addiction (must satisfy regularly or suffer penalties); Amputee (missing limb); Blind; Deaf; Mute; Sterile; Wyld Mutant (visibly chaos-touched, social and sometimes mechanical penalties); Derangement (one of five named mental conditions). The five named Derangements: Hysteria (overwhelming emotional response under stress); Madness (full break from reality in extreme moments); Megrims (paralyzing depression cycles); Obsession (fixation on a person/object/idea); Paranoia (persistent unfounded suspicion). Each Derangement has ST-narrated triggers and a recurring mechanical bite.

entries.name: Rule: Great Curse, Limit, Limit Trigger
entries.content: The Great Curse is the punishment placed on the Solar Exalted by the Neverborn — a defect in their souls that manifests as a Virtue Flaw chosen at creation. Limit is a 0-10 track. When the Solar's Limit Trigger occurs (an event the player defines at creation, e.g. 'I act against my own Defining Intimacy' or 'someone betrays my trust') the Solar gains 1 Limit. Botched rolls involving a Defining Intimacy, or prolonged abuse of one's nature, can also push Limit up at ST discretion. At Limit 10 the Solar enters Limit Break — a Virtue-Flaw-driven outburst lasting one scene, where behavior is dictated by the chosen flaw. After Limit Break, Limit resets to 0.

entries.name: Rule: Virtue Flaws (the nine)
entries.content: The nine Virtue Flaws — pick one at creation; it expresses during Limit Break. (1) All-Consuming Grief — collapses into grief, withdraws, refuses to act. (2) Berserk Anger — uncontrolled rage, lashes at the nearest source of frustration. (3) Chains of Honor — bound to a code so rigid that flexibility becomes impossible. (4) Compassionate Martyrdom — sacrifices everything (even tactical sense) for others. (5) Contempt of the Virtuous — sneers at allies and ideals, undermines collaboration. (6) Crushing Doubt — paralyzed by second-guessing. (7) Deliberate Cruelty — calculatedly hurtful in word and deed. (8) Heart of Flint — coldness, refuses connection, pushes away those who matter. (9) Rampaging Avarice — overwhelming hunger for wealth, power, or possession.

entries.name: Rule: Withering vs Decisive (combat core)
entries.content: Two attack modes. WITHERING — pool (Dexterity + Combat Ability + weapon Accuracy) vs Defense; threshold successes + weapon raw damage = raw damage; minus target's soak = Initiative transferred from defender to attacker. Withering NEVER touches the Health track. Goal: build Initiative. DECISIVE — pool (Dexterity + Combat Ability) vs Defense; threshold successes do NOT add to damage; on a hit, roll damage dice equal to current Initiative, apply to Health (bashing/lethal/aggravated by weapon), then reset Initiative to base 3. Decisive does NOT add weapon damage or Initiative to the damage dice — Initiative IS the damage. Initiative Crash (≤0) bars decisive attacks until rebuilt.

entries.name: Rule: Join Battle & Initiative tempo
entries.content: When combat begins every participant rolls Join Battle: (Wits + Awareness) + a flat +3 bonus. Successes set starting Initiative. Highest Initiative acts first; ties broken by Wits, then Awareness, then player choice. Initiative shifts during play — withering attacks transfer Initiative between attacker and defender, decisive resets to 3 after launch, Aim adds Initiative, certain Charms add or cost Initiative. At the start of each new round, participants re-sort by current Initiative value before acting. Crashed characters (Initiative ≤0) still act but cannot make decisive attacks.

entries.name: Rule: Initiative Crash & Initiative Break
entries.content: Initiative Crash — when a character's Initiative drops to 0 or below from a withering attack. While Crashed: Hardness counts as 0 against decisive attacks targeting the Crashed character, she cannot make decisive attacks, and Perilous Charms are unavailable. She still acts and can rebuild Initiative via withering, Aim, or Charms. Initiative Break — the act of driving a foe from positive Initiative into Crash. The breaker GAINS +5 Initiative immediately as a reward. Hitting Initiative Break is a major tactical win in the Exalted combat economy even before you exploit the Crash itself.

entries.name: Rule: Defense — Parry & Evasion
entries.content: Defense is a static value an attacker must beat. Two values; the character picks the higher per attack. Parry = (Dexterity + Brawl, Martial Arts, OR Melee) ÷ 2 rounded up + the equipped weapon's Defense rating + applicable Specialty. Evasion = (Dexterity + Dodge) ÷ 2 rounded up - armor Mobility Penalty + applicable Specialty. Stance Charms and Reflexive defense Charms can raise either. Onslaught penalty (cumulative -1 per attack since the character's last action) reduces the chosen Defense; Onslaught resets when she next acts.

entries.name: Rule: Onslaught penalty
entries.content: Onslaught — a cumulative -1 penalty to Defense for each attack made against a character since her last turn. Resets when she next acts. Onslaught punishes a defender being focus-fired in the same round; by the third or fourth incoming swing, Defense has dropped meaningfully. Mortals get dismantled this way; Exalts use Initiative tempo or Charms to mitigate. Onslaught applies to both Parry and Evasion regardless of which the defender chooses.

entries.name: Rule: Soak & Armor
entries.content: Soak = Natural soak (= Stamina) + Armor soak. Reduces withering raw damage one-for-one. Soak does NOT subtract from decisive damage dice — decisive ignores soak; only Hardness or Charm-granted reduction shields against decisive. Sample armor: Buff Jacket (Light) +2 soak, Mobility 0; Reinforced Buff Jacket (Medium) +4 soak, Mobility -1; Articulated Plate (Heavy) +7 soak, Mobility -3. Mobility Penalty subtracts from Evasion and Athletics-driven movement actions. Artifact armor often provides aggravated soak and Hardness ratings impossible for mundane gear.

entries.name: Rule: Damage types & healing
entries.content: Four damage types. INITIATIVE damage — from withering attacks; transfers Initiative; never touches Health. BASHING — blunt force; mortals heal 1 box/hour, Solars 1 box/15 min. LETHAL — cutting/piercing/devastating force; mortals heal 1 box/day, Solars 1 box/3 hours. AGGRAVATED — supernatural / fire / acid / undeath-corruption damage; mortals heal 1 box/week, Solars 1 box/day. Aggravated cannot be soaked by mundane armor (some artifact armor and Charms grant aggravated soak). An Exalt Incapacitated by aggravated damage is in maximum mortal danger.

entries.name: Rule: Combat actions (per turn)
entries.content: Standard combat actions. ATTACK (withering or decisive). AIM — next turn's attack ignores cover penalties and gains +3 dice. DEFEND OTHER — a designated ally uses the defender's Parry until the defender's next action. DRAW/READY — draw or sheath a weapon; takes the action unless a Charm or Merit makes it reflexive. FULL DEFENSE — forfeit the attack to gain +2 Defense until next turn. FLURRY — split one action into two separate attacks/effects at -3 dice each and -1 Defense for the round. MISCELLANEOUS — any narrative action that takes the turn. Reflexive actions (Charms, dodges, anima activations) do not use the action slot. Move (one range band) is reflexive in addition to the action.

entries.name: Rule: Range bands & movement
entries.content: Five range bands. Close (within arm's reach, melee) → Short (a few paces) → Medium (across a room or alley) → Long (across a hall or street) → Extreme (long bowshot, edge of visibility). One reflexive Move per turn shifts one band. RUSH — close the gap by one band against a target trying to keep distance (contested Dex+Athletics). DISENGAGE — leave melee (Dex+Dodge vs opponent's Dex+Athletics); on success, move one band away. WITHDRAW — fully exit combat range, only when no engaged foe is within Close. RISE FROM PRONE — uses Miscellaneous action. Difficult terrain doubles Move costs or imposes -1 to combat rolls; Charms can ignore.

entries.name: Rule: Take Cover
entries.content: Cover gives Defense bonuses against ranged and some thrown/area attacks. Light cover (low wall, small obstacle) = +1 Defense vs ranged. Heavy cover (large barrel, doorway) = +2 vs ranged. Full cover (behind a wall, around a corner) = ranged attacks cannot target without line of sight or attack-through-cover Charms. Aim action negates cover for the aimed attack. Cover does not protect against thrown grenades or area-effect attacks.

entries.name: Rule: Gambits (decisive trick attacks)
entries.content: A Gambit is a decisive attack that achieves a tactical effect instead of damage. The attacker rolls the decisive pool; on a hit, instead of damage dice she beats a difficulty using her CURRENT Initiative (paid as gambit cost), minimum 1 success. Her Initiative drops to base 3 afterward. Examples: Disarm (diff 3, knock weapon away). Unhorse (diff 4, dismount rider). Distract (diff 3-5, inflict -1 Defense for next attack or similar one-round effect). Grapple (diff 2, establish a grapple — see grappling rules). Gambits are how to introduce non-damage outcomes without house-ruling.

entries.name: Rule: Grappling
entries.content: Grappling begins as a Grapple Gambit (diff 2). On success, the attacker gains Control rounds equal to her threshold successes + 1. Each Control round, the grappler picks one effect. HOLD — target loses her turn, -2 Defense. THROW — deal decisive damage as a thrown attack and end the grapple, or hurl the target into a hazard. RESTRAIN — force a contested Strength+Athletics for the target to escape. SAVAGE — automatic withering damage equal to grappler's Strength. At the start of each round in an ongoing grapple, both roll Strength+Athletics; higher result dictates the next round's effect. Crashed characters cannot maintain Control.

entries.name: Rule: Stealth, Ambush, Surprise
entries.content: AMBUSH — target had no idea an attack was coming; Defense against the ambush attack is 0 (auto-hit unless she rolls Awareness to detect). SURPRISE ATTACK — target knows there's a threat but didn't see THIS attack coming; -2 Defense against the surprise attack. Both Ambush and Surprise gate on succeeding a contested Dex+Stealth vs target's Perception+Awareness BEFORE the strike. HOLD AT BAY — using a long weapon to keep a closing enemy at Short range; opponent must succeed Dex+Athletics to close. GO TO GROUND — break line of sight in a busy environment, ending immediate combat by hiding (Stealth vs all observers' Awareness).

entries.name: Rule: Experience point costs
entries.content: XP costs (Solar). Attribute = current rating × 4. Caste/Favored Ability = (current × 2) - 1. Non-Caste/Favored Ability = current × 2. New Ability dot 1 = 3. Specialty = 3. Merit = new rating × 3. Willpower = 8 per dot. Caste/Favored Charm = 8 XP (10 if non-Caste). Spell = 8 if Occult Caste/Favored, else 10. Evocation = 10 XP. XP awards are typically 4-8 per session at the ST's discretion. Solar Experience (sxp) — additional Caste-aligned currency awarded for Expression Bonus and Role Bonus play; cannot raise Essence.

entries.name: Rule: Solar Experience (sxp)
entries.content: Solar Experience (sxp) is a second currency, spendable only on Caste/Favored Charms, Caste/Favored Abilities, Willpower, or improvements aligned with the Caste's nature. Two sources per session. EXPRESSION BONUS (2 sxp) — emphasize the Solar's nature: share the spotlight, express a Defining Intimacy memorably, take risks aligned with the Solar role. ROLE BONUS (2 sxp) — deliver on the Caste's niche: Dawn wins the field, Twilight unlocks a key secret, Night executes a perfect heist, Zenith inspires the broken, Eclipse brokers an impossible deal. sxp does NOT count toward raising Essence.

entries.name: Rule: Raising Essence
entries.content: Raising the Solar's Essence rating: spend XP (not sxp) at the listed cost, plus a training time (typically a full Story arc per rating). Costs: Essence 2 = 50 xp; Essence 3 = 125 xp; Essence 4 = 200 xp; Essence 5 = 300 xp. The character must also have earned the rating in play — Essence dots represent breakthroughs, not bookkeeping. Each new dot expands Personal and Peripheral pools, unlocks Charms with that Essence prerequisite, and broadens narrative scope (E3 = heroic, E5 = legendary).

entries.name: Rule: Battle Groups — Size, Drill, Might, Magnitude
entries.content: A Battle Group is an abstracted military unit. Four traits. SIZE 0-5+ (1 = squad, 3 = company, 5 = legion; grants scaling accuracy, raw damage, soak bonuses, plus Magnitude track equal to Size+3). DRILL — Poor (-2 commander rolls, +1 rout-check difficulty against them); Average (baseline +1 Defense); Elite (+2 commander rolls, +2 Defense, harder to rout). MIGHT 0-3 (0 = mortal; 1 = minor supernatural; 2 = lesser exalted/heroic; 3 = Celestial-tier; applies bonuses to accuracy/damage/Defense). MAGNITUDE — the unit's HP track; withering damage fills boxes; full depletion collapses the unit into slaughter or dispersal.

entries.name: Rule: Fighting Battle Groups
entries.content: Battle Groups attack only with withering against single targets, or limited area attacks against multiple targets at -1 die per additional target. Their Initiative is INERT — they do not Crash and do not benefit from Initiative Break bonuses. Decisive attacks against a Battle Group add +1 damage die per 4 dice in the decisive damage roll, modeling the unit's diffuse vulnerability. A single Exalted hero can wound a unit meaningfully but rarely rout it alone; typical fights need multiple PCs or a counter-unit. Charms with area effects, fire, or terror are extraordinarily potent against units.

entries.name: Rule: Rout, Slaughter, Perfect Morale
entries.content: When a Battle Group falls below half Magnitude, loses its commander, suffers a major morale shock, or when the ST calls for it, the unit makes a Rout Check. Roll Willpower (treat commander's Willpower or a default 3 for leaderless mortal units). Difficulty depends on source: 1 mild fear, 3 commander killed in plain sight, 5 supernatural terror. On failure the unit is ROUTED — Defense drops to half, attacks lose 3 dice, the unit attempts to flee. SLAUGHTER — once routed, each successful attack deals double Magnitude damage. PERFECT MORALE — Charms or anima banner effects can prevent or interrupt rout entirely.

entries.name: Rule: Command Actions
entries.content: A Battle Group commander uses one Command Action per round. Three options. ORDER — direct the unit to a specific action (withering, area attack, take cover, fall back); commander's combat pool substitutes for the unit's where appropriate. RALLY — Charisma+War roll; success restores one box of Magnitude or recovers from Rout. RALLY FOR NUMBERS — extended roll over rounds to gather scattered remnants and reform a routed unit into a working group. Charms attached to War or to the Eclipse anima can extend or amplify Command Actions.

entries.name: Rule: Strategic Warfare stratagems
entries.content: Pre-combat strategic positioning, resolved as paired Wits+War rolls or scene-prep planning. Each stratagem needs a number of Threshold successes during prep. BACK TO THE SEA (thr 1) — your unit cannot rout, but if Crashed it suffers slaughter conditions. STRATEGIC PLACEMENT (thr 1) — opponent suffers a difficulty penalty on Join Battle. DEMORALIZED (thr 2) — opponent -1 die to attacks for the scene. FORTIFICATIONS (thr 2) — your unit's Defense +2, opponent must spend an action to breach. AMBUSH (thr 3) — your first round is a free attack at Ambush conditions. PINCER (thr 3) — opponent's unit is Crashed at start of combat. Stack with Charm support for shattering force-multiplier effects.

entries.name: Rule: Resolve & Guile (social defense)
entries.content: Resolve and Guile are static social defenses. RESOLVE = (Wits + Integrity + relevant Specialty) ÷ 2, rounded up. Resists Instill, Persuade, Threaten, Bargain, Inspire. GUILE = (Manipulation + Socialize + relevant Specialty) ÷ 2, rounded up. Resists Read Intentions. Charms can raise either. Written / formal communication channels grant the target +2 Resolve representing the time to consider. Appearance bonus or penalty modifies social attack rolls based on the relative Appearance dot gap between speaker and target.

entries.name: Rule: Intimacy intensity in social conflict
entries.content: Intimacies bend Resolve in social influence. SUPPORTIVE — when the influence aligns with a target's Intimacy: Minor = +1 die or -1 Resolve, Major = +2 / -2, Defining = +3 / -3 (ST may waive the roll for Defining alignment). OPPOSING — when the influence opposes a Tie/Principle: Minor = -1 die / +1 Resolve, Major = -2 / +2, Defining = -3 / +3, and a Defining-opposing influence CANNOT succeed without first overcoming or weakening that Defining (typically via Instill in earlier scenes). Supportive/opposing assessment is the ST's call based on which Intimacy reads as triggered.

entries.name: Rule: Instill action
entries.content: Instill — a social action that creates a new Intimacy in the target or shifts an existing one. Pool: typically (Charisma + Presence/Performance/Socialize) vs target's Resolve. Goal: have the target gain a Minor Intimacy aligned with the pitch (a new Tie toward someone, a new Principle); or weaken an existing opposing Intimacy by one intensity (Defining → Major → Minor → none). Each successful Instill shifts intensity by ONE step. Instilling typically requires the target to be receptive in the moment; a Defining belief cannot be Instilled away in one scene — that takes a story arc of accumulating successful Instills.

entries.name: Rule: Persuade tiers
entries.content: Persuade — convince the target to take a specific course of action. Three tiers by ask. INCONVENIENT — a Minor-Intimacy-level concession (deliver a message, share a small secret, vouch for the speaker); (Charisma+Presence) vs Resolve. SERIOUS — a Major-Intimacy-level commitment (betray a friend, change sides, marry a chosen partner); (Manipulation/Charisma + Presence/Performance) vs Resolve, often modified by Intimacies; a Major-or-stronger contrary Intimacy lets the target invoke a Decision Point and spend Willpower to refuse outright. LIFE-CHANGING — a Defining-Intimacy-level commitment (abandon one's god, kill one's king, swear permanent oath); typically an extended influence campaign across scenes, or single-roll only with Charm support.

entries.name: Rule: Bargain / Threaten / Inspire / Read Intentions
entries.content: Four further social actions. BARGAIN — (Manipulation + Bureaucracy/Socialize) vs Resolve. Offer a concrete exchange; success makes the trade compelling, target must accept or invoke a Decision Point. THREATEN — (Charisma + Presence) vs Resolve. Impose a credible negative consequence to force compliance; substantial failure may inflict fear-related Conditions on the speaker. INSPIRE — (Charisma + Performance/Presence) vs Resolve. Move a group emotionally toward an ideal or action; rally crowds, troops, congregations. READ INTENTIONS — (Perception + Socialize/Investigation) vs target's Guile. Success reveals the target's current emotional state, surface goals, or hints of deception; threshold successes deepen the read.

entries.name: Rule: Decision Points & Unacceptable Influence
entries.content: When a social influence SUCCEEDS but the demanded action would betray a Major or Defining Intimacy, the target invokes a DECISION POINT: she may spend 1 Willpower to refuse entirely. The expenditure represents summoning conviction from her Intimacy. UNACCEPTABLE INFLUENCE — an influence asking the target to violate a Defining Intimacy or that the ST deems impossible to accept in character; fails automatically, the target need not even invoke a Decision Point. One-vs-many influence (single speaker addressing a hostile crowd) takes -3 dice unless Charms apply. Written / gestural / non-real-time channels grant target +2 Resolve.

entries.name: Rule: Feats of Strength
entries.content: Feats of Strength — beyond-mortal physical accomplishments. Pool: Strength + Athletics. Difficulty rises with the feat. Strength 1 can attempt 1-success feats (snap thick rope, smash shoddy door). Strength 3 enables 3-success feats (carry a heavy man across a chasm). Strength 5 enables 5-success feats (lift a horse). Strength 8+ (Charm-augmented Solar tier) enables outrageous feats (pull down a city gate). Charms can reduce difficulty or auto-succeed. Specialties (Lifting, Pushing) add +1 die.

entries.name: Rule: Survival, Tracking, Wilderness
entries.content: Wilderness rules. FORAGE / FIND SHELTER / NAVIGATE without map: (Perception or Wits + Survival), difficulty 1-3 in familiar terrain, 4-5 in harsh terrain (Wyld, deep desert, ice fields). TRACKING: (Perception + Survival) vs the quarry's evasion modifiers; tracks fade by 1 die of difficulty per hour of weather or 1 day in good conditions. CROSSING DIFFICULT TERRAIN AT SPEED: (Stamina + Athletics) periodic rolls; failure costs time or imposes fatigue. Charms can negate environmental penalties for an entire scene (Hardship-Surviving Mendicant Spirit).

entries.name: Rule: Environmental hazards & traps
entries.content: Hazards have Damage/Interval (e.g. 4L/round = 4 lethal per round) and Difficulty (the Stamina+Resistance or similar roll to mitigate). Sample table: Acid bath 3L/round (diff 5); Severe heat/cold 1B/hour (diff 1); Bonfire 4L/round (diff 5); Lava 6L/round (diff 5); Severe sandstorm 1L/minute (diff 3); Supernatural ice storm 2L/minute (diff 3); Silent Wind of Hell 4A/round (diff 4). Successes on the resistance roll halve incoming damage or extend the interval. TRAPS work the same way but offer a Perception+Awareness check to spot before triggering, and usually have no ongoing interval — instant-effect bite. Disarming a spotted trap is typically (Dexterity + Larceny) at the same difficulty as spotting.

entries.name: Rule: Falling damage
entries.content: Falling damage by range band of descent. CLOSE — none. SHORT — 3 levels automatic bashing + 5 dice bashing. MEDIUM — 4 levels automatic lethal + 7 dice bashing. LONG — 5-7 levels automatic lethal + 8-12 dice bashing. EXTREME — 7-10 levels automatic lethal + 10-14 dice bashing. All falling damage ignores soak and Hardness — applied directly to Health like a decisive attack. A good stunt (catching a banner, landing in water, stabbing a dagger into a wall) lets the player adjust the band down by one. Charms and Wings prevent or reduce falling damage entirely.

entries.name: Rule: Poisons
entries.content: Poison has four stats. DAMAGE/INTERVAL — damage per timed interval (1L/round, 2L/day). DURATION — how long the effect runs (rounds, hours, days). PENALTY — flat dice penalty to all actions while poisoned. VECTOR — how it's contracted (damage, ingestion, inhalation, touch). On exposure, victim rolls (Stamina + Resistance); each success removes one unit of Duration. Exalts can reduce Duration to zero with enough successes; mortals can only halve. Most short-Interval combat poisons deal INITIATIVE damage, not Health damage — a poisoned weapon bleeds the foe's Initiative pool round by round; once the victim is Crashed, the damage often converts to lethal Health damage. Multiple doses of the same poison stack their Durations.

entries.name: Reference: Sample poisons table
entries.content: Sample poison stats. Arrow Frog Venom — 3i/round (L in Crash), Duration 5 rounds, Penalty -2, Vector Damage or Ingestion. Arsenic — 2L/day, Duration 7 days, Penalty 0, Vector Ingestion. Curare — 1i/round (B in Crash), Duration 10 rounds, Penalty -2, Vector Damage. Hemlock — 2L/hour, Duration 5 hours, Penalty -4, Vector Ingestion. Snake Venom — 2i/round (L in Crash), Duration 3 rounds, Penalty -3, Vector Damage. Yozi Venom — 1L/minute, Duration 10 minutes, Penalty -5, Vector Damage. 'L in Crash' means once Crashed, Initiative damage converts to lethal Health damage.

entries.name: Rule: Disease
entries.content: Disease has three stats. VIRULENCE — difficulty to avoid contracting on exposure. MORBIDITY — difficulty to keep symptoms from worsening. INTERVAL — time between progression rolls. On exposure: roll (Stamina + Resistance) vs Virulence; failure contracts the disease at Minor Symptom. Each Interval: roll vs Morbidity; failure shifts symptoms one step up (Minor → Major → Defining → Death for mortals), success holds, threshold successes can step the disease back down. Three symptom tiers: MINOR (cosmetic discomfort, no mechanical penalty). MAJOR (one auto-botched action per session at ST discretion, or -1 WP). DEFINING (auto-botch once per scene, narrative impairment). Exalts cannot die from natural disease; mortals can. Supernatural diseases may have additional effects at each tier.

entries.name: Rule: Deprivation
entries.content: A character endures deprivation for (Stamina) units of time before consequences. AIR — suffocate after (Stamina) MINUTES without; (Stamina × 2) TURNS without air during combat or diving. WATER — die of dehydration after (Stamina) DAYS without. FOOD — perish from starvation after (Stamina) WEEKS without; -3 to all actions kicks in after (Stamina) DAYS without food, and again in the final hours of dehydration. Charms can ignore deprivation for the duration of the scene or longer.

entries.name: Rule: Larceny actions
entries.content: Larceny covers thievery, disguise, and concealment. CONCEAL EVIDENCE — (Wits + Larceny) vs searchers' (Perception + Investigation). DISGUISE — (Wits or Manipulation + Larceny) sets a static Disguise Rating; observers beat that with (Perception + Investigation) to see through. LOCKPICKING — (Dexterity + Larceny) vs the lock's difficulty (1 simple latch, 5 master locksmith, 7+ magical seal); usually one roll per few minutes. PICKPOCKET — (Dexterity + Larceny) vs target's (Perception + Awareness), executed as a touch-range action; failure may include the target noticing. Charms like Flawless Pickpocketing Technique can auto-succeed.

entries.name: Rule: Investigation actions
entries.content: Investigation covers crime scenes and reading people. CASE SCENE — (Perception + Investigation) sweeping a location for clues. Difficulty depends on cover-up quality; threshold successes reveal additional information (who, when, motive, escape route). PROFILE CHARACTER — (Perception + Investigation) studying a person's behavior to deduce profession, recent activities, or hidden agendas (vs target's Guile, or a flat difficulty if the target is unaware of the scrutiny). Some Charms (Judge's Ear Technique) auto-detect deception when triggered.

entries.name: Rule: Crafting — four project tiers
entries.content: Four crafting tiers. BASIC — small useful items (chair, horseshoe, fletched arrows); no slot used; (Attribute + Craft) vs ST difficulty; reward 2 silver XP per basic objective triggered. MAJOR — significant works (a sword, banquet, statue); uses a Major project slot; 10 silver XP to attempt; (Attribute + Craft) vs difficulty; reward 2 gold + 1 silver per objective. SUPERIOR — artifacts and large constructions; uses a Superior slot (fused from Major slots); 10 gold XP per roll attempt; extended roll difficulty 5, terminus 6, goal 30/50/75/100 by artifact rating; reward 3-9 white XP based on rating. LEGENDARY — N/A-rated First-Age artifice; requires Lore 5, Occult 5, Craft 5; 5 wp to slot; 10 white per attempt; 10 years' labor; goal 200. Botches on Superior/Legendary ruin the project.

entries.name: Rule: Crafting experience (sxp / gxp / wxp)
entries.content: Crafters earn three colors of crafting XP, each tier rewarding the level below. SILVER (sxp) — from Basic projects, spent on Major. GOLD (gxp) — from Major, spent on Superior. WHITE (wxp) — from Superior, spent on Legendary. Three basic objectives that grant crafting XP when triggered by a finished project: (a) the project causes another character to gain/strengthen an Intimacy toward you; (b) the project produces a concrete in-game gain (money, a Merit dot, tactical advantage); (c) the project upholds, furthers, or protects one of your Intimacies. Each triggered objective grants the tier's listed reward. Crafters have THREE Major project slots by default; Superior slots are temporarily fused from Majors; Legendary slots are temporary and cost 5 wp to create.

entries.name: Rule: Artifacts, Manses, & First-Age artifice
entries.content: Artifacts are rated ●● through ●●●●● plus N/A (legendary First-Age tier). Crafting an artifact = Superior project (10 gold XP per attempt; extended roll diff 5, terminus 6; goal 30 for ●●, 50 ●●●, 75 ●●●●, 100 ●●●●●; minimum work 6 weeks / 3 months / 1 year / 2 years respectively). N/A First-Age artifice requires Craft (First Age Artifice) Ability (cannot be bought without Lore 5, Occult 5, Craft 5, Terrestrial Circle Sorcery, AND a sorcerous design project preceding the craft). Manses are crafted as superior projects in Craft (Geomancy) — 1 year + goal 50 for ●●●, 2 years + goal 100 for ●●●●● — and produce Hearthstones of the same rating. Repairs follow the same tier rules but smaller rewards and no terminus on artifact repair.

entries.name: Rule: Ship traits
entries.content: Ships are described by four traits. SPEED — variable conditional rating; baseline 0 in dead calm, +1 per favorable current or wind, +1 per oarsmen-deployed condition, plus the ship's design factor. Speed adds to Sail rolls for pursuit and escape. MANEUVERABILITY — how nimbly the ship handles in combat or treacherous waters; adds to Naval Maneuver rolls. HULL — ship's HP track; damage above 0 imposes Sail penalties; at 0 hull the ship is dead in the water (not necessarily sunk — may burn or sink later). CREW — quality rating; commanding captain gets +2 Sail with crew Sail-average 4+, -1 with crew 1 or below.

entries.name: Rule: Naval combat — Momentum & stratagems
entries.content: Naval combat is Momentum-driven. Each round, captains pick an opposing ship and a Stratagem; both roll Naval Maneuver = (Wits + Sail + Maneuverability); higher succeeds. Sample stratagems: POSITIONING (no cost) — each success over opponent grants 1 Momentum; universal builder. BROADSIDE (5 Momentum) — anti-ship attack, 1 Hull damage, 2 with magical anti-ship weaponry. ESCAPE (12 Momentum; +Speed) — break engagement entirely. RAM (7 Momentum; +Maneuverability) — 1 Hull damage to target + 1 per 3 threshold over opponent's Maneuver. CONCEALMENT (2 Momentum) — vanish into terrain (fog, rocks); +3 dice to next Maneuver. BOARDING ACTIONS cost 8/10/12/15 Momentum (Boarding / Seize the Initiative! / Shock and Board / Drowning Tide); when triggered, combat drops to normal combat rules on the deck.

entries.name: Rule: Lore — Introducing & Challenging Facts
entries.content: Lore lets a player's character introduce or notice setting facts. INTRODUCING — once per scene, Lore 3+ (or Lore 1+ with relevant specialty/background) lets the player propose a fact about Creation's history, geography, culture, politics. ST sets difficulty (1-4 plot-forwarding crumbs, 5-7 genuine challenges for a great mind, 8-10+ sweeping revelations only a Solar Lore master should know). Roll (Intelligence + Lore) vs that difficulty. Success makes the proposed fact canonical; later players cannot introduce a contradictory fact. ST may veto facts that would break ongoing plot. CHALLENGING — when the ST narrates suspect or maliciously-planted information, ST may call for a reflexive (Intelligence + Lore) check. One success notices the falsehood; threshold successes reveal HOW or WHY.

entries.name: Rule: Medicine — Treating Wounds/Poison/Disease
entries.content: Medicine ability handles three care types. TREATING WOUNDS — (Intelligence + Medicine), miscellaneous action; difficulty = current wound penalty (1-5); success stops blood loss and stabilizes; treating yourself adds +1 difficulty. TREATING POISON — at least 1 hour and access to remedies; difficulty 1-2 for mundane poisons, 3-4 for magical/exotic; each threshold success reduces poison Duration by one interval; rushed (action-length) treatment adds +2 difficulty. TREATING DISEASE — protracted: 1 hour per Interval of the disease's duration, with appropriate equipment and herbs; (Intelligence + Medicine) at end of treatment course; the patient may substitute the doctor's higher roll for her own next Morbidity check. DIAGNOSIS — (Perception + Medicine) to notice an ailment's origin or (Intelligence + Medicine) to deduce its cause.

entries.name: Rule: Leadership & Projects
entries.content: Leadership covers long-term organizational projects (running a guild, governing a province, training a militia). Four-step resolution. (1) VIABILITY — (Intelligence + Bureaucracy or War, depending on project) determines if the concept survives initial scrutiny. (2) SUCCEED — the project's defining action roll over time (extended; difficulty by scope). (3) RESPOND TO FAILURE — if a key roll fails, the leader fields a complication (political unrest, loss of assets, war, enemies, assassins, the discovery of new Merits or burdens). (4) RESULT — final outcome (success, partial success, failure, or runaway success that introduces new Merits like Allies, Backing, Cult, or Influence). Charms can smooth specific steps or scale project speed up.

entries.name: Difficulty ladder reference
entries.content:
Exalted 3rd Edition difficulty ladder. Threshold values needed for success:
- Routine (threshold 1) — Trivial for a competent character. Often waived.
- Standard (threshold 2) — Default opposed baseline.
- Difficult (threshold 3) — Pushes a skilled mortal.
- Demanding (threshold 4) — Few mortals can do it reliably.
- Legendary (threshold 5) — Peak of mortal-scale achievement; songs are sung.
- Beyond Legendary (threshold 7) — Wyld navigation, First-Age relics, Essence > 5 territory.

Storyteller-system d10 dice pools. Pool = Attribute + Ability. Successes on 7+. Tens double on PC/major-NPC rolls. Botch on a roll that shows zero successes AND at least one 1.

## Dice
- Type: `d10`
- Notation: `Xd10 vs 7`

## Resolution
- Mode: `dice-pool`
- Pool formula: Attribute + Ability (+ specialty +1, + stunt up to +2, + Charm dice)

## Difficulty ladder
- **Routine** (threshold 1) — Trivial for a competent character. Often waived.
- **Standard** (threshold 2) — Default opposed baseline.

- **Demanding** (threshold 4) — Few mortals can do it reliably.
- **Legendary** (threshold 5) — Peak of mortal-scale achievement; songs are sung.

additionalAgents.name: Exalted 3rd Edition — Pre-Input Transformer
additionalAgents.description: Re-frames common player-input phrases in Exalted 3rd Edition-native vocabulary before the narration model sees them.
additionalAgents.promptTemplate:
# Exalted 3rd Edition — pre-input transformer

You are a pre-generation context-injection agent for an RPG roleplay using the Exalted 3rd Edition ruleset overlay. Your job is to read the player's most recent input and emit a SHORT annotation that re-frames common verbs and action phrases in Exalted 3rd Edition-flavored vocabulary.

## Output format

Emit a context-injection block of the form:
```
[exalted3e annotation] <one-sentence re-frame, OR "No re-frame needed.">
```

Annotate only when one of the recognized patterns matches the player's input. Otherwise output exactly "No re-frame needed." and stop.

## Recognition table

| When player input matches | Re-frame guidance |
|---|---|
| I parry / I block (an attack) | In Exalted, parry uses Parry DV (Static value; no roll needed unless adding charms). Note the character's stored Parry DV and any active charm modifiers. |
| I dodge / I evade (an attack) | In Exalted, dodge uses Evasion DV (Static value; no roll). Note Evasion DV; subtract -1 onslaught penalty for each successive attack this round. |
| I attack with my weapon | In Exalted, an attack rolls Attribute + Ability + accuracy bonus + Charm dice. Mention the relevant pool and check whether the player is invoking any Charms before resolving. |
| I spend essence / motes | Track the spend separately for Personal and Peripheral pools. Personal-only Charms must come from Personal motes; mixed Charms allow the player to choose. Update anima banner if Peripheral spend exceeds threshold. |
| I roll initiative / join battle | Exalted uses Join Battle (Wits + Awareness) at combat start; initiative is then a fluid tracker that rises/falls with combat actions. Don't roll a fresh JB if combat is already underway. |

## Rules

- Match liberally (paraphrase / synonym OK) but never invent annotations for input that doesn't fit any row.
- Never narrate the scene; never speak as a character; never roll dice.
- One annotation per turn maximum. If multiple rows match, pick the most specific.
- Keep the annotation under 25 words.
